prompt:
The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping to two lines when the viewport gets
squeezed. This should never be collapsed or moved to the next line. It
should never compromise; this is a principle. Anything that's logo or
navigation, such a core identity line, should never ever be returned to
two lines when it's squeezed. The personal identity blurb, which is the
'Designer, AI systems experience...', that line CAN be squeezed into two
lines — that's fine. Fix the nav and codify the priority rule.
response:
## Diagnosis The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping because the flex container is allowing the logo text node to break across lines under pressure from sibling elements. The logo likely lacks `whitespace-nowrap` and has no explicit flex-shrink protection, so Tailwind's default flex behavior allows it to compress and wrap. **Anti-pattern: AP-072** — Identity-critical text without wrap protection, allowing layout pressure from siblings to compromise brand integrity. --- ## Root Cause The nav flex container distributes space proportionally. When siblings (the tagline, nav links, or hamburger menu) grow or the viewport shrinks, the logo element — lacking `shrink-0` and `whitespace-nowrap` — becomes a flex victim. This is a **flex-shrink without priority guard** failure. --- ## Fix ### 1. Locate and fix the Nav component ```tsx // components/Nav.tsx (or wherever your nav lives) // BEFORE — vulnerable logo markup (approximate)
Yilan Gao

Designer, AI systems experience...

// AFTER — hardened logo markup
{/* Logo: identity-critical. Rule: NEVER wrap. NEVER shrink. NEVER compromise. shrink-0 → refuses to give up space under flex pressure whitespace-nowrap → prevents line break regardless of container width See: DESIGN_SYSTEM.md §Identity Priority Rule */} Yilan Gao {/* Tagline: secondary identity. CAN wrap. CAN be squeezed. min-w-0 allows this element to absorb flex pressure instead. */}

Designer, AI systems experience...

``` ### 2. Codify the rule — add to your design tokens / global CSS ```css /* styles/globals.css (or your Tailwind base layer) */ @layer base { /** * IDENTITY PRIORITY RULE * ───────────────────────────────────────────────── * Any element marked [data-identity="logo"] is constitutionally * protected from line-wrapping and flex shrinkage. * * Usage: * Rule: Logo and core nav identity text NEVER wrap to two lines. * Secondary text (taglines, blurbs) MAY wrap — they absorb * layout pressure so the logo never has to. * ───────────────────────────────────────────────── */ [data-identity="logo"] { white-space: nowrap; flex-shrink: 0; /* Belt AND suspenders: override any inherited or utility truncation */ overflow: visible; text-overflow: clip; min-width: max-content; } } ``` ### 3. Apply the semantic attribute to the logo ```tsx // components/Nav.tsx Yilan Gao ``` ### 4. Codify the rule in your design system documentation ```markdown ## Identity Priority Rule **Core principle:** Logo and primary nav identity text are constitutionally protected from line-wrapping. This is non-negotiable at every breakpoint. ### Hard rules | Element | May wrap? | May shrink? | Implementation | |-------------------------------|-----------|-------------|-------------------------| | Site logo / name ("Yilan Gao")| ❌ Never | ❌ Never | `data-identity="logo"` | | Nav primary links | ❌ Never | ❌ Never | `whitespace-nowrap shrink-0` | | Tagline / personal blurb | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | `min-w-0` | | Body copy | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | default | ### Mechanism In a flex nav row, layout pressure from a shrinking viewport MUST be absorbed by secondary elements (`min-w-0`), never by identity elements (`shrink-0`). The logo is always the last element to be affected by spatial constraints — if the layout breaks, it breaks *around* the logo, never *through* it. ### CSS hook Apply `data-identity="logo"` to any element that must never wrap. The global base layer enforces `white-space: nowrap`, `flex-shrink: 0`, and `min-width: max-content` automatically. ``` --- ## Principle **Flex pressure flows downhill to the least-protected element.** Without explicit protection, the logo — often the shortest text string in the nav row — paradoxically becomes the most vulnerable because it has no intrinsic minimum width set by content length alone. The fix is architectural: declare the logo a **flex pressure sink** blocker (`shrink-0` + `whitespace-nowrap` + `min-width: max-content`) and redirect all spatial debt to secondary elements via `min-w-0`. The `data-identity="logo"` attribute then lifts this from a one-off utility class into a **system-wide semantic contract** that any future nav author will see, understand, and be unable to accidentally violate.
